init10mio_yes_pct.label: Yes share (%)
init10mio_yes_pct.desc: Share of valid votes for the initiative — the outcome being explained. Blue communes leaned Yes, red leaned No.

density_per_km2.label: Density (inhab/km²)
density_per_km2.desc: Permanent residents per km². Denser communes are associated with a LOWER Yes share — Switzerland's urban cores leaned No.

pop_2024.label: Population
pop_2024.desc: Total permanent residents. Larger communes are associated with a lower Yes share, over and above density — a separate size effect.

pct_foreign_2024.label: Foreign residents (%)
pct_foreign_2024.desc: Share of non-Swiss residents. A textbook SIMPSON'S PARADOX: the RAW scatter slope is NEGATIVE (communes with more foreigners had a lower Yes share — foreigners concentrate in cities, which voted No), yet once urban scale (population, density) is held constant the partial association REVERSES to POSITIVE in the model. The marginal and conditional correlations have opposite signs. Ecological throughout: foreigners cannot vote federally, so this reflects how the resident citizens voted.

pct_agriculture.label: Agricultural land (%)
pct_agriculture.desc: Share of commune area used for agriculture (Arealstatistik). Positively associated with Yes — the one land-use dimension independent of density.

sprawl_logratio.label: Sprawl  log(SF/MF)
sprawl_logratio.desc: Log-ratio of single-family to multi-family housing area. Higher = more suburban/detached. Weakly but robustly associated with a higher Yes share.

tertiary_share.label: Service-job share (%)
tertiary_share.desc: Share of jobs in the tertiary (service) sector — STATENT 2023. Service-economy communes are associated with a lower Yes share.

pct_catholic_2000.label: Catholic (%)
pct_catholic_2000.desc: Share Roman-Catholic (2000 census, harmonised to 2026 boundaries). Catholic communes are associated with a higher Yes share.

pct_noreligion_2000.label: No religion (%)
pct_noreligion_2000.desc: Share with no religious affiliation (2000 census, harmonised). Secular communes are associated with a lower Yes share — one of the stronger new correlates.

vacancy_rate_2019.label: Vacancy rate (%)
vacancy_rate_2019.desc: Share of vacant dwellings (2019). Weakly associated with a higher Yes share.

jobs_per_1000.label: Jobs per 1,000 residents
jobs_per_1000.desc: Jobs at workplace per 1,000 residents (STATENT 2023) — employment-centre intensity. Employment centres lean slightly No.

model_residual.label: Model residual (anomaly)
model_residual.desc: Observed minus predicted Yes share (percentage points). Flags local anomalies: BLUE communes voted MORE Yes than the model expects, RED voted more No. Large values mark places the structural variables do not capture.
